messages.text: How would you characterize the quality of Jahez's FY2025 revenue growth?
Revenue growth was real but low-quality: the top line held up while gross margin gave way.

events.text:
**Revenue growth was real but low-quality: the top line held up while gross margin gave way.**

Net revenue reached SAR 2,323.6M, up 4.7% YoY[[1]], but the mix is what matters:

- **Commission revenue** grew 16.3% to SAR 1,113.8M[[2]], lifting the implied group take rate to roughly 15.4% (commission ÷ GMV)[[3]], the monetization engine is working.
- **GMV** expanded 10.8% to SAR 7,245.2M[[4]], so end demand is intact.
- But **gross margin compressed to 22.8%**, from 24.4% a year earlier[[5]], delivery and logistics costs outran the take-rate gains.

Net: healthy monetization, softening unit economics. The quality question is really a margin question, which the P&L answers next.

events.quote: Net Revenue 2,323.6 (+4.7% YoY), Group Financial Summary FY2025
Take Rate = Commission Revenue / GMV (glossary definition)

messages.text: Then why did net income fall so hard?
Net income attributable to shareholders fell 61.2% to SAR 73.0M, but most of the drop is one-time, not structural.

events.text:
**Net income attributable to shareholders fell 61.2% YoY to SAR 73.0M**[[1]], and most of the drop is one-time, not structural.

- **~SAR 55.4M of Q4 one-offs** account for the bulk: a Red Color impairment (12.2), Marn goodwill impairment (11.8), an Others-segment ECL charge (23.4), and ~8.0 of Snoonu deal expenses[[2]].
- **Adjusted EBITDA** still fell 22.8% to SAR 193.0M[[3]], so the operating base did soften.
- **D&A rose 75.4% to SAR 89.4M**[[4]] as the platform capitalised more, a real drag below EBITDA.
- Loss-making segments compounded it: Others net income −SAR 82.5M and Non-KSA −SAR 33.9M[[5]].

Strip the one-offs and the franchise is closer to flat-to-down than collapsing, but the margin trend is the thing to underwrite.

events.quote: Net Profit attributable to shareholders of the parent 73.0 (-61.2% YoY)
Q4 one-offs: Red Color 12.2m impairment, Marn goodwill 11.8m, Others-segment ECL 23.4m, Snoonu deal expenses ~8m
Depreciation & Amortization 89.4 (+75.4% YoY), Adj. EBITDA reconciliation
Net Income (Shareholders): Others (82.5), Non-KSA (33.9), segment table FY2025

messages.text: @screening walk me through the screening verdict on Darb.
Recommendation: advance to a pitch meeting. Five criteria pass, one concentration flag. The call is yours.

events.text:
**Recommendation: advance to a pitch meeting. Five criteria pass, one concentration flag.** The call is yours.

- **Sector mandate: Pass.** Logistics SaaS sits inside the Technology & Consumer mandate[[1]].
- **Ticket & stage: Pass.** The SAR 40M Series B falls within the SAR 20–60M ticket band and the mandated Series A–C window[[2]].
- **Shariah pre-screen: Pass.** Business activity is permissible, with no leverage red flags at pre-screen[[3]].
- **Concentration: Warn.** Technology & Consumer exposure is 8.5% of AUM today; a SAR 40M ticket (2.0% of AUM) would take post-deal sector exposure to 10.5%, just above the 10% cap[[4]].

Red flags, conflicts, and related-party checks are clean. The only open item for the committee is whether to accept a temporary 0.5pt breach of the sector cap or trim the ticket.

events.quote: Ticket band SAR 20–60M; stage window Series A–C
Single-sector concentration cap of 10% of AUM

title.ar: التحضير لاجتماع لجنة الاستثمار يوم الاثنين

messages.text: What's ready for Monday's Investment Committee?
Two names are committee-ready, and the Jahez deep-dive is in flight.

events.text:
**Two names are committee-ready, and the Jahez deep-dive is in flight.**

- **Thara Pay**, analysis complete. Implied IRR of 18.5% clears the 15% mandate hurdle, with a mid risk score of 5/10 and a clean Shariah screen[[1]]. It's the strongest risk-adjusted case on the table.
- **Jahez**, deep-dive underway. The headline the committee will press on is FY2025 net income of SAR 73.0M, down 61.2% YoY[[2]], largely one-off, but worth pre-empting.

Three Lunar-branded artifacts are drafted from the model and awaiting your review before circulation: the **IC memo**, the **board deck**, and the **valuation workbook**. Every figure traces to a cited source, and each answer is written to the audit trail.

events.quote: Implied IRR 18.5% vs 15% hurdle; risk score 5/10; Shariah pass
Net Profit attributable to shareholders 73.0 (-61.2% YoY)
